DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE 10772F FINAL ENVIRONMENTAL STATEMENT . -) - 2- 71 FOR PROPOSED CASTAIC-HASKELL JUNCTION POWER TRANSMISSION LINE (FOREST SERVICE, REGION 5) SUMMARY SHEET This is an administrative action related to the proposed Castalc- Haskell Junction power transmission line. The action consists of issuing a prelicense special use permit Z. for right-of-way for the subject transmission line to allow con- struction prior to issuance of FPC license for Project 2426. The line will be a part of that licensed project. The State of California and Los Angeles County are particularly affected. Effects on the environment are largely visual impacts on the Angeles National Forest. Some visual impact in the vicinity of Castaic Dam is unavoidable. Some short-term erosion and soil disturbance can be expected during and right after con- struction. Eleven alternate routes were considered. The route selected is considered to cause least environmental im- pact. Many alternative routings were considered (see Project Location Map in draft statement). Written comments on the draft statement were received from the Federal Power Commission, the Bureau of Outdoor Recre- ation and the Board of Supervisors, Los Angeles County. The draft statement was made available to the CEQ on April 1, and to the public on April 9, 1971. The final statement is made available to CEQ and the public (pursuant to the provisions of the Freedom of Information Act) on May 20, 1971. - 157E U. S. DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE FINAL ENVIRONMENTAL STATEMENT Prepared in Accordance With Sec. 102 (2) (c) of P. L. 91-190 May 17, 1971 Proposed Castaic-Haskell Transmission Line Nature of Proposed Action The City of Los Angeles Department of Water and Power, a joint ap- plicant for license with the State of California Department of Water Resources for the California Aqueduct (F. P. C. Project No. 2426) before the Federal Power Commission has requested the necessary authorization to proceed with the construction of the Castaic-Haskell Junction primary transmission line. At least two circuits of the pro- posed four circuits must be completed by July 1971 to enable water delivery through the Castaic Power Plant for the purpose of meeting the State's water delivery commitments to Southern California water agencies. Approximately 3.3 miles of this 11.5 mile transmission line is to be located within the Angeles National Forest. The city has applied for a prelicense special use permit, for the right-of-way through the National Forest. The Department of Water and Power work force is currently engaged in constructing the line on private lands outside the Forest boundary. It is geared to commence construction within the Forest as soon as a right-of-way is granted. The Forest Service proposes to issue the requested permit. Statement The Department of Water and Power, City of Los Angeles (hereinafter called LADWP) has prepared an environmental statement on the pro- posal. A copy of that statement is enclosed with the draft statement, dated March 25, 1971. The Forest Service concurs in that statement as correctly describing the probable environmental effects of the project. These are summarised as follows: Environmental Impact. The local environmental impact a of the proposed project will substantially be one of esthetics. This impact has been reduced to a minimum through careful routing. The selected route does not pass directly through populated areas or po- tential recreation sites. Less than 30 percent of the route would be visible to the general public. There will be some erosion and soil disturbance, but this can be mitigated by stringent application of erosion control techniques. (See LADWP Report). Favorable Environmental Effects. As part of the larger b. scheme for Project No. 2426, the line will induce favorable environ- mental effects outside the project area. Currently, the Department of Water and Power generates the majority of the power used by the City of Los Angeles at oil and gas fired steam generating plants within the Los Angeles basin. With the current concern over air pollution, the advantages of hydroelectric power are obvious. In addition to re- ducing the actual steam power generation during peak power demand periods, the availability of this hydro power also negates a part of the need for "spinning reserve" which normally approximates 25 percent of current load. This "spinning reserve" consists of steam plants in operation with no power load, being held in reserve for unexpected power demands or to cover power outages in the system. By eliminating the fuel consumption of this "spinning reserve," a considerable mone- tary saving can be realized along with a commensurate reduction in air pollution. Adverse Environmental Effects that Cannot be Avoided. C. Some visual impact in the vicinity of Castaic Dam is unavoidable. (See LADWP Report). Alternatives to the Proposed Action. (See LADWP Report). d. Relationship Between Local Short-term Uses of Man's e. Environment and the Maintenance of Long-term Productivity. Es- tablishment of the powerline will not materially affect the maintenance and enhancement of long-term productivity of National Forest lands. (See LADWP Report). Irreversible or Irretrievable Commitment . Resources. ſ. No resources will be irreversibly and irretrievably committed should the proposed action be implemented. (See LADWP Report) Consultation With Appropriate Federal Agencies. This work has been closely coordinated with the Federal Power Com- mission. Prelicense permit will contain whatever stipulations are required by both the Forest Service and the Commission. Review by State and Local Agencies. The proposed transmission line location is the result of over two years of considerable correspondence and numerous meetings involving the project proponents, the State of California and Los Angeles County Department of Parks and Recreation, the Los Angeles County Forester and Fire Warden, the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors, private landowners in the project vicinity, and the Forest Service. The location proposed in this report has been found acceptable by these agencies. The criteria used in establishing the route involved minimizing the inherent visual pollution, reducing the impact on all resources, and avoiding high value development lands and public highways while providing a route least likely to seriously curtail or hinder the use of aircraft in fire suppression activities and maintain- ing the applicant's power reliability requirements. The alternate routes studied are illustrated on the Project Location Map contained in the draft statement. Comments on Draft Statement. Written comments on the draft environmental statement were received from the Federal Power Commission, the Bureau of Outdoor Recreation, and the Board of Supervisors, Los Angeles County. Since none of these comments were of an adverse nature, the draft statement need not be revised. Copies of omments are enclosed herewith. Enclosures FEDERAL POWER COLOMI- HOW E. WASHINGTON, L.C. 20:26 IN REPLY HELL LA TO Octo Project No. 2426- California Department of Water Resources, State of California Department of Vater and Power of the City of Los Angeles Chief of the Forest Service APR 14 1971 Department of Agriculture Washington, D. C. 20250 Dear Sir: This is in response to the letter 2720(7100) dated March 31, 1971 from Associate Deputy Chief, Richard F. Droege requesting our comments on the U.S. Department of Agriculture draft environmental statement on the Castaic-Haskell Junction Transmission Line proposed to be constructed as part of the California Aqueduct Project No. 2426. Application for license for Project No. 2426, filed by the Department of Water Resources, State of California and the Department of Water and Power of the City of Los Angeles, is still before the Presiding Examiner. The hearing in the proceeding in Project No. 2426 was concluded in December of 1969. Inasmuch as the Commission has not yet acted on the li- cense application in this proceeding, it would be inappropriate to comment substantively on the draft environmental statement. However, I am of the opinion that in the alignment and design of the Castaic-Haskell Junction Line, Applicants have essentially followed the procedures and "Guidelines for the Protection of Natural, Historic, Scenic, and Recreational Values in the Lo- sign and Location of Rights of Way and Transmission Facilities" as set forth in Commission Order No. 414 issued November 27, 1970. t With respect to item b. Favorable Environmental Effects appearing on page 2 of the USDA Scatement, it might be pointed out that the pre-license construction and operation of the first two 230-kv circuits of the subject transmission line will be davoted less to the generation of significant emounts of peaking capacity or spinning reserves than to testing and operation of a 50-mw unit in the Castaic Power Plant as an energy absorbe: to allow safe delivery of project water through the Project's Angeles Tunnel, scheduled to commence October 15, 1971. Delivery of this water would be made to the Metropolitan Water District for use in its supply of water to Los Angeles and other municipalities and jurisdictions in populous southern California. A final evaluation of the environmental impact of tha Castaic-Haskell Junction Transmission Line by the Commission will be made in connection with its action on the license application for the entire California Aqueduct Project No. 2426.
